conditions.display: Unspecified sprain of right wrist, initial encounter
conditions.workInjuryDetails: Slipped on wet floor at construction site at 09:00, fell on outstretched right hand. Employer: Greenfield Construction Ltd, 55 Industrial Ave, Ottawa.

imaging.result: No acute fracture or dislocation. Soft tissue swelling around the radial styloid. Mild degenerative change at the first CMC joint.

clinical_assessment.physical_exam: Right wrist: marked soft tissue swelling dorsal and radial aspects. Tenderness over radial styloid, anatomical snuffbox, and volar wrist crease. Grip strength right 2/5 (severely reduced), left 5/5. ROM — Flexion: 30° (normal 80°); Extension: 20° (normal 70°); Radial deviation: 10° (normal 20°); Ulnar deviation: 20° (normal 30°). No neurovascular deficit. Capillary refill normal bilaterally. Finkelstein test positive. No bony crepitus. Volar wrist splint applied.
clinical_assessment.pre_existing_conditions: Chronic low back pain (M54.5, onset 2024-11-01) — may slow overall rehabilitation
clinical_assessment.diagnosis_description: Right wrist sprain/strain (S63.40XA). No fracture on X-ray. Soft tissue swelling radial styloid region.

not_able_to: use right upper extremity for sustained tasks
functional_abilities.other_limitations: Must wear volar wrist splint at all times including at modified work. No standard transmission driving. Avoid tasks requiring bilateral grip or vibrating tools.

referrals.reason: Wrist rehabilitation post-sprain, ROM restoration, strengthening
referrals.address: 200 Laurier Ave W, Ottawa ON K1P 6M8

referrals.reason: Functional abilities assessment if not improving in 4 weeks

encounters.type: Urgent office visit — WSIB initial assessment (Form 8)
encounters.notes: WSIB initial assessment. Injury date: 2026-05-27 at 09:00. Mechanism: slipped on wet concrete floor at job site, fell on outstretched right hand. Patient occupation: Construction Labourer. Employer: Greenfield Construction Ltd, 55 Industrial Avenue, Ottawa ON K1G 0Z3, phone 613-555-0980, supervisor Tom Bradley. Pain 6/10 rest, 9/10 activity. Physical exam: right wrist swelling, tenderness radial styloid and anatomical snuffbox, ROM markedly restricted (see clinical_assessment), grip strength 2/5. Diagnosis: Right wrist sprain/strain S63.40XA. No fracture on X-ray (X-ray completed same visit). X-ray findings: no fracture, soft tissue swelling radial styloid, mild first CMC degenerative change. Pre-existing: chronic low back pain — may impact recovery. Treatment: volar wrist splint applied; Naproxen 500 mg BID x 2 weeks; Cyclobenzaprine 10 mg qhs PRN x 1 week. Investigation: X-ray right wrist 2 views ordered and completed. Referral: Physiotherapy — Ottawa Physio Centre 613-555-0700, appointment 2026-06-03. RTW: discussed YES. Cannot resume regular construction duties. Modified duties from 2026-05-29 — no lifting >2 kg right hand, no gripping tools, no keyboard, no heavy equipment. Functional abilities: see rtw_plan. Limitations apply approximately 8-14 days; reassess at follow-up 2026-06-11. WSIB claim initiated. Billing: service code 8M, WSIB provider ID 0512345678, HST reg 812345678RT0001, service date 2026-05-28.

specialist_notes.summary: Patient commenced CBT May 2026. 6-session plan. Initial focus on cognitive restructuring and sleep hygiene. Patient engaged and motivated. Return to work planning to begin session 4.

functional_status.reason: Unable to manage classroom stress; concentration difficulties; sleep disturbance; anhedonia; low energy
functional_status.restrictions: Avoid high-stimulation, noisy environments. No supervisory or administrative duties initially. Graduated return: part-time 50% hours for 4 weeks, then full if tolerating.
functional_status.treatment_active: Sertraline 100 mg daily; Clonazepam 0.5 mg PRN; CBT with Dr. R. Singh PhD (6-session plan, started 2026-05-01, sessions every 2 weeks)
functional_status.functional_limitations: Sustained concentration limited to 20-30 minutes. Social interaction causes anxiety exacerbation. Fatigue prevents full-day activity. Sleep: initial insomnia, waking 3-4×/night. Appetite: reduced 30% from baseline. ADLs: maintained but effortful.
functional_status.able_to_do: Self-care ADLs, light housework, drive short distances, shop briefly
functional_status.not_able_to_do: Classroom teaching, managing 25+ students, lesson planning for sustained periods, administrative tasks, public presentations

Diagnosis: Major depressive disorder recurrent moderate (F33.1) and Generalized Anxiety Disorder (F41.1). Totally disabled from regular occupation as elementary school teacher. CBT initiated 2026-05-01 with Dr. R. Singh PhD (6-session plan). Active medications: Sertraline 100 mg daily, Clonazepam 0.5 mg PRN. Lab: iron deficiency (ferritin 14), Vitamin D deficiency (38 nmol/L) — supplements started: ferrous gluconate 300 mg daily, Vitamin D3 2000 IU daily. Patient not malingering — genuine impairment of concentration, emotional regulation, and stamina. Expected duration of disability: minimum 3 months; reassess 2026-07-15 for graduated RTW planning. Physician certifies patient is unable to perform the essential duties of her regular occupation and any occupation for which she is reasonably suited. Disability form completed for Manulife, policy ML-OCSB-2024-001. Employer: Ottawa Catholic School Board, 570 West Hunt Club Road, Nepean ON K2G 3R4, 613-224-2222.

medications.name: Hydrocortisone cream 1%
medications.frequency: twice daily to affected areas as needed

allergies: Cats — allergic rhinitis and asthma exacerbation

imaging.result: FEV1 74% predicted, FEV1/FVC 0.73. Post-bronchodilator FEV1 88%. Significant bronchodilator response. Pattern consistent with moderate-severe persistent asthma.

specialist_notes.summary: Confirmed peanut and tree nut anaphylaxis. Subcutaneous immunotherapy not indicated for food allergy. Allergen avoidance education reinforced. Referred for school anaphylaxis management plan. Considering dupilumab if atopic dermatitis not controlled on topicals.

school_medical_plan.triggers: Pollen (tree/grass/ragweed), cat dander, cold air, exercise in cold weather, upper respiratory infections

emergency_medications_at_school.name: EpiPen 0.3mg (epinephrine auto-injector)
emergency_medications_at_school.location: Kept with student AND in school office (2 devices)
emergency_medications_at_school.instructions: Inject outer right thigh immediately if anaphylaxis suspected. Call 911. Give second dose if no improvement in 5-10 min.

emergency_medications_at_school.name: Salbutamol (Ventolin) MDI 100mcg
emergency_medications_at_school.instructions: 2 puffs for asthma symptoms. If no relief in 10 min call 911.

emergency_medications_at_school.name: Benadryl (diphenhydramine) 25mg
emergency_medications_at_school.instructions: May give for mild allergic reaction after EpiPen.

school_medical_plan.gym_restrictions: Exempt from outdoor gym on high pollen days (Environment Canada pollen count > 3 moderate). Indoor gym permitted. Pre-treat with Salbutamol 2 puffs 15 min before vigorous exercise.
school_medical_plan.food_restrictions: Strict peanut and tree nut free zone in classroom. Student must not share food. Check all snack labels.
school_medical_plan.anaphylaxis_signs: Hives, throat tightening, difficulty breathing, vomiting, dizziness, pale skin
school_medical_plan.after_epinephrine: Call 911 immediately after use. Student must go to hospital even if feeling better.
school_medical_plan.peak_flow_action: Green zone >80% = normal school activity. Yellow 50-79% = use Ventolin, restrict activity, notify parent. Red <50% = use Ventolin, call 911.
school_medical_plan.follow_up: Annual review of plan with physician. Next review: September 2026.

Guardian: Patricia Okafor (mother) 613-555-0922. Referral to allergy (Dr. B. Wallace) already in place.
